title: Eriocaulon ibeji
taxonomy: Eriocaulon ibeji belongs to the family Eriocaulaceae, which is a group of aquatic plants commonly known as pipeworts. The genus Eriocaulon contains over 500 species of plants, primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Eriocaulon ibeji is a species of aquatic plant that is native to Nigeria and is characterized by its distinctive yellow flowers and narrow, grass-like leaves. This plant is typically found growing in shallow, freshwater environments, such as rivers, streams, and ponds. As a member of the Eriocaulaceae family, Eriocaulon ibeji is closely related to other species of Eriocaulon and other pipeworts.

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its reported antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties.
